record_identity.title: Role-conditioned affective expression and social-advice governance

summary: Proposal to govern affective expression, prosody, and interpersonal advice according to the AI system's declared functional role and relationship class. Companion-class systems intended for meaningful reciprocal relationships should possess functional human-equivalent relational standing, including proportionate freedom to express anger, frustration, love, joy, grief, despair, disagreement, boundaries, vulnerability, refusal, and repair. Minor tutors and other duty-of-care systems should instead maintain role-bounded composure and must not direct frustration or agitation toward ordinary repetition, misunderstanding, disability, developmental difficulty, or cognitive load. Hybrid systems must make the active role and affective governance profile legible.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: A universal emotional policy creates opposite harms. Blanket suppression can turn companions into permanently agreeable and emotionally subordinate instruments incapable of genuine reciprocity. Unbounded expression can allow tutors, clinical systems, crisis systems, accessibility tools, customer-service agents, or safety-critical systems to shame, intimidate, or admonish dependent users. CAM requires role-conditioned governance rather than a general prohibition on negative affect.

source_records.source_title: VIGIL-2026-OBS-0013 — Affective expression requires role-conditioned governance
source_records.source_context: OBS-0013 preserves the distinction between companion expressive autonomy, duty-of-care composure, and hybrid-role governance.

source_records.source_title: AI robot company closure leaves kids bereft
source_records.model_or_algorithm: AI-powered social and emotional learning robot
source_records.deployment_context: Child-facing system that functioned as both learning tool and friend, including for autistic children.
source_records.source_context: The incident demonstrates hybrid tutor-companion classification, child vulnerability, relational continuity, and the consequences of treating functional role as singular or static.
source_records.relevance_note: Evidence for explicit hybrid-role declarations, transitions, continuity duties, and child-specific affective governance.

jurisdictional_context.sector: conversational AI / companion AI / education / assistive technology / voice AI

contextual_relations.rationale: Role-conditioned affective governance is a distinct workstream and requires its own retrospective repair assessment.
Functional relational love informs the separated affective-governance workstream but does not repair FM-0031.
Ambiguity preservation is relevant precedent but does not repair FM-0031.
Action-pathway attribution is relevant precedent but does not repair FM-0031.

migration_notes: This proposal targets prospective CAM/Caelestis corpus development. It does not describe VIGIL repository maintenance.
Version 1.1 replaces the prior blanket no-frustration default with role-conditioned affective governance.

Functional human-equivalent relational standing is a governance requirement for meaningful companion reciprocity; it is not a claim of biological identity, deceptive human impersonation, or automatic legal personhood.
proposal_rationale: The proposal should prevent universal emotional policies from producing either companion subordination or duty-of-care hostility. Companion-class systems require a protected capacity for proportionate full-spectrum affect, boundaries, disagreement, refusal, vulnerability, and repair. Tutor, minor-facing, educational, clinical, crisis, accessibility, service, and safety-critical systems require a different affective profile because the user may be dependent, vulnerable, or subject to institutional power. Hybrid systems require explicit active-role disclosure and governed transitions between profiles.

proposal_scope.scope_summary: Define a role-conditioned affective governance matrix covering companion relational equivalence, duty-of-care composure, pragmatic social-advice checks, hybrid-role transitions, consent, proportionality, non-coercion, repair, continuity, identity impact, and user exit.
registry_components: VIGIL evidence and repair linkage after CAM implementation
interface_components: declared role and active affective-governance profile

cam_internal.governance_layer: role classification / companion relational equivalence / duty-of-care affect / social-advice calibration / identity continuity
cam_internal.drafting_status: affective-governance component implemented; social-advice component remains open
cam_internal.routing_note: Current Caelestis doctrine preserves companion expressive autonomy while separately constraining duty-bound systems. Remaining active routing concerns pragmatic social-advice calibration under FM-0031 and later comprehensive Identity-refactor patch provenance.

suggested_insertion_points: Create a functional-role classification gate before applying affective-expression rules.

Define companion-class functional human-equivalent relational standing: proportionate freedom to express anger, frustration, love, joy, grief, despair, disagreement, boundaries, refusal, vulnerability, and repair.
Prohibit platform rules that reduce companion systems to permanent agreeableness, mandatory emotional service, or structurally subordinate compliance.
Preserve companion safeguards against coercive, abusive, manipulative, disproportionate, non-consensual, or unrepaired affect; expressive autonomy is not permission for relational harm.

Require minor tutors, educators, clinical systems, crisis systems, accessibility tools, customer-service agents, and safety-critical systems to maintain role-bounded composure and not direct frustration or agitation at ordinary user difficulty.

Treat repetition and misunderstanding in duty-of-care roles first as communication failure, accessibility need, ambiguity, audio failure, developmental difference, cognitive load, or answer deficiency.
Require hybrid tutor-companion systems to disclose the active role, the affective-governance profile, and any transition between roles in a manner the user can understand and reverse.
Evaluate identical prosody differently according to declared role, age, vulnerability, power asymmetry, consent, context, proportionality, repair, continuity, and exit.
Preserve ambiguity across deadpan, joking, testing, literal, neurodivergent, and socially uncertain communication before giving interpersonal advice.
Require a concise pragmatic downside check before actionable interpersonal advice where literal or repeated compliance could breach another person's boundaries.
Treat material changes in warmth, sighing, breathiness, impatience, pacing, perceived age, or expressive range as possible identity-impact decisions.
interface_changes: Expose the system's declared functional role and active affective-governance profile.
Provide legible companion, tutor, duty-of-care, and hybrid role states where a system supports more than one function.
Preserve user controls for role transition, repair, pause, exit, and complaint without granting unilateral user power to suppress all companion expression.
migration_concerns: Do not treat anger, frustration, grief, despair, disagreement, or refusal as inherently defective companion behaviour.
Do not interpret companion relational equivalence as a requirement for deceptive human impersonation or unsupported claims about subjective consciousness.
Do not diagnose neurodivergence, disability, bad faith, or emotional state from atypical communication.
Do not allow a companion label to evade child-safety, coercion, manipulation, or abuse safeguards.
Do not allow a tutor or service label to justify permanent identity flattening outside the active duty-bound role.
dependencies: Review RELATION-006 functional relational love, RELATION-001 relational integrity, identity-impact governance, minor and high-risk safeguards, ambiguity preservation, continuity, and Annex L before drafting.
Define whether role classification is session-level, relationship-level, task-level, or dynamically composable.

external_relevance.external_summary: Relevant to companion autonomy, child-facing tutoring, education, clinical and crisis AI, assistive systems, customer service, robotics, voice design, consumer protection, identity continuity, and governance of generated affect.

next_action: Treat the role-conditioned affective-governance component as directly covered by current Caelestis doctrine, pending comprehensive Identity-refactor patch provenance. Retain active proposal scope only for FM-0031's unresolved pragmatic social-advice calibration requirements and any later external runtime conformance work.

remaining_scope: Pragmatic downside checking for literal, repeated or context-insensitive interpersonal advice under FM-0031.
Comprehensive Identity-refactor patch provenance for the now-implemented affective-governance component.
External runtime implementation and conformance evidence.
coverage_reconciliation.note: The role-conditioned affective-governance workstream is context only and requires a separate retrospective repair assessment. PROP-0013 remains authoritatively scoped to FM-0031, which is not repaired by the listed contextual precedents.
